title: Dad's Drift Lesson
objective: Survive a 2-4 minute driving lesson by entering the correct colored gates, obeying changing lesson calls, and building the highest combo before you run out of warnings.

controls.outcome: The tractor hops into the next crop lane.

controls.input: Touch and hold while moving across lanes
controls.outcome: The tractor slides sideways and repaints itself to the lane color it enters.
The tractor enters cleanly and satisfies straight-driving lesson calls.

steps: Start in one of three dirt lanes with a tractor color already active.
Colored gates roll down the field; pass through a gate only if your tractor color matches it.
Repaint by drifting into a new lane before the next gate reaches you.
Every few seconds Dad shouts a lesson: either "Drive it straight" or "Let it slide."
For the next few gates, you must either enter cleanly or still be drifting when you cross, changing the safest route.
A wrong color, a missed lesson, or clipping a hay bale costs one warning; lose all three and the run ends.
scoring: Each correct gate gives points. Consecutive correct gates raise a combo multiplier up to x6. Obeying Dad's active lesson adds a bonus burst, so the best scores come from matching color and driving style at the same time.
tips: Drift early when you need both a lane change and a repaint.
When Dad switches lessons, stop chasing the nearest gate and plan two gates ahead.
Use straight entries to protect a long combo; use drift entries to rescue bad color setups.

trendContext.title: My dad taught me to drive tractors 20 years ago. I don’t think this is quite what he had in mind... 🚜💨 Testing the drift physics for my browser game. What do you guys think? #TractorDrift #IndieDev #Gaming #WebDev
